context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the phoenix-channels-essentials instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always authenticate in `connect/3`** — tokens must be verified; channels bypass the Plug pipeline

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `handle_in` for client-to-server, `push` for server-to-client, `broadcast` for server-to-all**

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use Presence for tracking connected users** — don't roll your own presence tracking

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Generate a token server-side after user authentication → see [Step 2](#step-2--generate-tokens-server-side)

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Verify the token in `connect/3`; if auth fails, confirm salt matches between `sign` and `verify` and `max_age` hasn't expired → see [Step 3](#step-3--verify-tokens-in-the-socket)

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use token-based authentication — session-based auth is unavailable in channels.

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Define a Presence module once per app, then track users in `handle_info(:after_join, ...)`:

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use `Phoenix.ChannelTest` to test socket connections, joins, and message handling:
